headline: Renewed strikes put commercial shipping at risk in the Strait of Hormuz
dek: The IMO had recorded 58 confirmed regional maritime incidents and 17 seafarer deaths by 17 July. Another 6,000 seafarers were stranded aboard vessels unable to leave the Gulf safely.

headline: NATO commits to two years of Ukraine support at Ankara summit
dek: Alliance leaders pledged €70 billion in military support for 2026 and at least the same level in 2027 as UN monitors recorded June’s highest monthly civilian toll since April 2022.

headline: Governments face a costly wave of debt refinancing
dek: The OECD expects governments and companies to borrow $29 trillion from bond markets in 2026. Refinancing accounts for 78 per cent of projected borrowing by OECD governments.

headline: Data-centre demand is testing power grids and utility forecasts
dek: The IEA projects global data-centre electricity use could nearly double by 2030. Utilities must decide who pays for new capacity when large customer forecasts prove wrong.

headline: How printers and riders carried the Declaration across the colonies
dek: The Declaration reached the public through broadsides, newspapers and public readings. Its distribution in 1776 shows how a political text acquired force through circulation.

headline: The Sun Also Rises in its 100th year: expatriates, war and Pamplona
dek: Published on 22 October 1926, Hemingway’s first novel will reach its centenary this autumn. Its routes through Paris and Pamplona still shape the expatriate myth.

headline: Spain beat Argentina in extra time to win the 2026 World Cup
dek: Ferran Torres scored in the 106th minute of a 1–0 final. Spain conceded one goal in eight matches and won its second men’s World Cup.

headline: Dostoevsky’s Underground Man, resentment and the imagined audience
dek: The 1864 narrator invents hostile readers, rehearses humiliation and uses self-knowledge without changing his conduct. The pattern gives the novel a precise connection to online argument.

headline: The 48-team World Cup drew record crowds and raised workload questions
dek: FIFA reported 6.8 million spectators and 308 goals across 104 matches. Independent data on travel, player recovery and public cost will determine the fuller case for expansion.
